postmanEvidence: newman@6.2.2 differential evidence covers file, binary, and multipart form-data attachment transport for imported file references.
Installed Postman Desktop runtime/source audit records the parent-owned file-binding policy classification.

observations: PostMeter preserves imported file, binary, and form-data src references and exposes them through the script-visible Postman request body facade.
Scripts can use only source references that have a workspace-reviewed local file binding. Script-created arbitrary paths fail closed before the parent sender reads any file.
The script worker never receives local file contents, host file handles, filesystem APIs, Node fs, or arbitrary local path privileges.
The parent sender reads approved regular files only at send time and applies the documented request body size cap; oversized attachments remain governed by the audited production resource policy.
The Newman differential fixture compares brokered file, binary, and multipart form-data attachment behavior against newman@6.2.2.
